decisions.title: Approved RedDirt Environment Fallback
decisions.decision: Calendar-local environment values take precedence; approved missing values may be loaded server-side from RedDirt environment files.
decisions.boundary: RedDirt values are allowlisted and never exposed to client code.

decisions.title: Netlify Uses Injected Environment Variables
decisions.decision: Production deployment uses Netlify environment variables rather than filesystem fallback.

decisions.title: Separate Public and Server Environment Schemas
decisions.decision: Browser-safe and server-only variables use separate validation and access paths.

decisions.title: Safe Structured Logging
decisions.decision: All application logging passes through redaction-aware structured logging.

decisions.title: Security Headers Before Authentication
decisions.decision: Establish security headers and request safety before login is implemented.

decisions.title: Honest Capability Reporting
decisions.decision: System status distinguishes configured, enabled, complete, and production-ready.

decisions.title: No Distributed Rate-Limit Claim
decisions.decision: Any in-memory limiter is development-only and must not be described as distributed production protection.

decisions.title: Candidate Data Remains Prohibited
decisions.decision: Real candidate schedule information remains prohibited until authentication, permissions, and calendar schema protections are complete.

decisions.title: Standing Availability Policy Before Schema
decisions.decision: Encode weekday work-unavailable blocks, vacation override capability, and Tuesday Little Rock default as policy before database events exist.
decisions.boundary: No database event rows are created in Step 3.

decisions.title: Federated Command Calendar
decisions.decision: Build one application with a Command Calendar roll-up and specialized calendar workspaces (travel, communications, social, county, staff, etc.).
decisions.reason: Teams need owned areas while leadership needs a single operational truth and cross-calendar conflict detection.

decisions.title: Many-to-Many Event Calendar Membership
decisions.decision: Canonical events relate to calendars through memberships (primary + connected), not a single calendar_type field alone.

decisions.title: Multi-Level Calendar Permissions
decisions.decision: Enforce calendar-, event-, and section-level access including AVAILABILITY_ONLY, with default-deny for protected fields.
decisions.boundary: AVAILABILITY_ONLY / limited viewers receive SafeCalendarEventView (calendar name, safe title, general location, times) — never private notes, people, logistics, or strategy. Busy-only is reserved for protected personal / security-sensitive cases.

decisions.title: Configurable Command Roll-Up
decisions.decision: Each calendar defines roll-up mode: FULL_DETAIL, TITLE_ONLY, BUSY_ONLY, MILESTONES_ONLY, or DO_NOT_ROLL_UP.
decisions.reason: Prevent Command Calendar unreadability from minor team activity.

decisions.title: External Calendars Never Authoritative
decisions.decision: Imported/synced external events retain origin and sync status; Command Calendar remains internal master.

decisions.title: Occupied Time Must Remain Visible
decisions.decision: Protected events remain visible as occupied calendar blocks to authenticated campaign users.
decisions.reason: Campaign teams must understand candidate availability even when details are restricted.

decisions.title: Calendar Name Is Always Operational Context
decisions.decision: Limited event representations display the event’s primary calendar name.
decisions.reason: The calendar name explains the nature of the commitment without exposing sensitive details.

decisions.title: Safe Event Titles Are Visible by Default
decisions.decision: Authenticated campaign users normally see a safe event title.
decisions.boundary: Sensitive events may substitute a restricted title.

decisions.title: General Location Is Visible When Safe
decisions.decision: Limited viewers see a general location when available.
decisions.boundary: Exact private or security-sensitive locations must be generalized or hidden.

decisions.title: Server-Side Event Sanitization
decisions.decision: Unauthorized event fields are removed before data reaches the client.
decisions.reason: Client-side hiding is not a security boundary.

decisions.title: Visibility Is Layered
decisions.decision: Access is resolved through authentication, roles, teams, calendar permissions, event overrides, section permissions, sensitivity, and location disclosure.

decisions.title: One Event, Multiple Calendars — Primary Label
decisions.decision: A canonical event may belong to multiple subcalendars, but one primary calendar supplies the default category label for limited display.
decisions.boundary: Additional memberships are not exposed when they would reveal protected strategy.

decisions.title: Historical Calendar Floor
decisions.decision: First historical Google Calendar import begins November 1, 2025.

decisions.title: External Events Enter Through Staging
decisions.decision: Google events must be fetched, normalized, deduplicated, reviewed, and approved before canonical persistence.

decisions.title: Public iCal and API Provider Contracts
decisions.decision: Support public iCalendar ingestion and a future OAuth-backed Google Calendar API source.

decisions.title: External Identity Preservation
decisions.decision: Preserve Google event IDs, iCal UIDs, recurrence identities, and source fingerprints.

decisions.title: No Blind Historical Claims
decisions.decision: Imported events do not prove Kelly attended until reviewed.

decisions.title: Structured Entry Before Free Text
decisions.decision: Prefer dropdowns, checkboxes, presets, and structured controls for calendar entry.

decisions.title: Every Event May Carry a Full Operational Plan
decisions.decision: Events may include objectives, program flow, staffing, packing, travel, communications, and follow-up.

decisions.title: AI Suggestions Are Evidence-Based and Selectable
decisions.decision: AI may recommend values using reviewed patterns; every suggestion requires explicit human selection.

decisions.title: Reviewed History Powers Future Recommendations
decisions.decision: Only reviewed and approved historical events may serve as trusted pattern sources.

decisions.title: Drafts Are Clearly Non-Live
decisions.decision: Step 3 drafts and imports must be visually and technically distinguished from live calendar events.

decisions.title: Dedicated PostgreSQL Schema
decisions.decision: All Kelly Calendar-owned tables live inside schema kelly_calendar on the shared RedDirt database server.
decisions.boundary: No RedDirt-owned objects may be altered by Kelly Calendar migrations.

decisions.title: One Canonical Event
decisions.decision: A real-world commitment has one canonical event associated with multiple calendars via memberships.

decisions.title: Command Calendar Is a Roll-Up Surface
decisions.decision: The Command Calendar derives from subcalendar memberships and roll-up policies rather than duplicating event records.

decisions.title: Event Details Are Section-Protected
decisions.decision: Permissions may differ for basic details, travel, communications, fundraising, files, and protected notes.

decisions.title: Historical Imports Require Approval
decisions.decision: Google Calendar records enter canonical history only through reviewed, transactional approval.

decisions.title: External Identity Is Preserved in Database
decisions.decision: Imported events preserve external IDs, iCal UIDs, recurrence identities, and source fingerprints in kelly_calendar tables.

decisions.title: Optimistic Concurrency
decisions.decision: Event updates use version checking to prevent silent overwrites (409 EVENT_VERSION_CONFLICT).

decisions.title: Operational Plans Are Structured Data
decisions.decision: Program flow, packing, staffing, communications, travel, and follow-up are normalized event records.

decisions.title: Templates Are Snapshotted
decisions.decision: Applying a template copies or snapshots its version so later template edits do not rewrite historical events.

decisions.title: Audit Records Are Redacted
decisions.decision: Audit logs preserve accountability without storing secrets or unrestricted sensitive content.

decisions.title: Reference Seeds Are Idempotent
decisions.decision: System calendars, counties, templates, and default views may be seeded repeatedly without duplication.

decisions.title: Step 5 Schema Without Step 4 Keeps Mutations Off
decisions.decision: Database schema may be applied before auth is complete, but live mutations and candidate-data entry stay disabled until Step 4 gates pass.

decisions.title: Operational Intelligence Precedes Final UI
decisions.decision: Workflow, readiness, conflict, timeline, and recommendation logic are built before the final mobile Command Calendar interface.

decisions.title: Deterministic Rules Before AI
decisions.decision: Explicit campaign rules generate recommendations before probabilistic AI is used.

decisions.title: Workflow Applications Are Versioned
decisions.decision: Applied workflows preserve definition version and do not change when the source workflow is later edited.

decisions.title: Readiness Is Explainable
decisions.decision: Every readiness score includes domain weights, missing items, blockers, and next actions.

decisions.title: Critical Blockers Override Numeric Scores
decisions.decision: An event cannot be labeled ready while a critical safety, travel, approval, or compliance blocker remains.

decisions.title: Suggestions Require Human Approval
decisions.decision: Workflow expansions and recommendations are previews until explicitly accepted.

decisions.title: Historical Patterns Use Reviewed Evidence
decisions.decision: Only reviewed and eligible historical records may contribute trusted operational patterns.

decisions.title: Conflicts Are Advisory
decisions.decision: Conflict detection may identify and recommend, but may not move, cancel, or alter events autonomously.

decisions.title: Readiness and Completion Are Separate
decisions.decision: Preparation readiness and post-event completion use separate calculations.

decisions.title: Command Summaries Are Permission-Filtered
decisions.decision: Operational summaries use the same protected projection and section-permission system as individual event views.

decisions.title: Authenticated Actor Comes Only From the Server Session
decisions.decision: Client input may never identify the mutation actor; actors resolve only from the app-signed HTTP-only session.
decisions.note: Burt Step 5.6 script listed this as ADR-061; numbering continues after Step 5.5 ADRs.

decisions.title: Authorization Is Action-Based
decisions.decision: Routes authorize named actions against resources instead of scattering role-name checks.

decisions.title: Authentication and Authorization Are Separate
decisions.decision: A valid session proves identity but does not automatically grant access; default remains deny.

decisions.title: Mutation Responses Use Safe Projections
decisions.decision: Successful writes return only fields the acting viewer may access.

decisions.title: Operational Intelligence Applies Through Ordinary Mutations
decisions.decision: Workflow and recommendation acceptance may not bypass event authorization, version checks, validation, or audit.

decisions.title: Candidate Data Remains Disabled After Technical Unlock
decisions.decision: Technical mutation readiness does not itself authorize real Kelly data.

decisions.title: Netlify Fails Closed Without Session Secret
decisions.decision: Production authentication may not use a development fallback when APP_SESSION_SECRET is missing.

decisions.title: Production Authentication Requires an Explicit Runtime Secret
decisions.decision: The deployed application must fail closed when APP_SESSION_SECRET is missing or invalid.
decisions.note: Burt Step 5.7 script listed related items as ADR-068–073; repository continues numbering after Step 5.6 ADRs.

decisions.title: Deployment Proof Uses Synthetic Data Only
decisions.decision: Production-environment verification may not require real candidate information.

decisions.title: Proof Mode Cannot Bypass Security
decisions.decision: Deployment-proof tooling must exercise the real authentication, authorization, versioning, transaction, audit, and projection layers.

decisions.title: Operator Acceptance Is a Separate Gate
decisions.decision: Automated technical validation cannot substitute for operator review and sign-off.

decisions.title: Step 6 Depends on Deployed Mutation Proof
decisions.decision: The mobile command shell may not begin against unproven production authentication and mutation behavior.

decisions.title: Proof Records Must Be Isolated
decisions.decision: Synthetic deployment-proof records must not affect real operational summaries, county metrics, candidate workload, sync, or public surfaces.

decisions.title: Import Field Precedence on Fingerprint Change
decisions.decision: On re-import when an external fingerprint changes, local operator edits win for title, notes, and status; source timing wins only when the imported Event has never been manually rescheduled.
decisions.boundary: Does not authorize Google write-back or silent Mission mutation.

decisions.title: ICS Feeds Are Private and Signed
decisions.decision: Calendar subscription feeds (CC-10) are private and signed; no public anonymous subscription URL.
decisions.boundary: Public filtered calendars require a separate RFC and aggressive redaction.

decisions.title: CC-08 Ships Time Grid Before Drag-and-Drop
decisions.decision: Advanced Day/Week Scheduling Workspace delivers a true time grid and click-to-edit first; drag-and-drop is a follow-on.
decisions.boundary: Does not block click-to-edit reschedule already owned by Event lifecycle.

decisions.title: Feed Redaction of Private Residences
decisions.decision: ICS and subscription feeds redact exact private or residential locations; feeds may expose CITY or BUSY_ONLY equivalents only.
decisions.boundary: Internal Event sheet visibility remains governed by existing visibility doctrine.

decisions.title: Source-Deleted Events Remain CANCELLED History
decisions.decision: When an external source deletes an event previously applied to KCCC, the canonical Event remains as CANCELLED history with provenance rather than being hidden or hard-deleted.
decisions.boundary: Does not auto-create Missions or invent schedule replacements.

decisions.title: Calendar Completion Program Locked CC-01 through CC-12
decisions.decision: The primary engineering track is Calendar Completion CC-01…CC-12 (Burt Option C). Unrelated campaign expansion is paused. Communications OS remains frozen.
decisions.boundary: CC-05 and CC-06 remain gated by Usability Synthesis or explicit Kelly/Steve waiver.

decisions.title: CC-01 Provenance Contracts Reusable by CC-02 Without Combining Deliverables
decisions.decision: CC-01 builds import approve/merge/reject first and designs provenance/audit contracts for CC-02 reuse, but does not combine the Integrity Console into CC-01.
decisions.boundary: CC-01 success is the decisive single-Event apply/idempotency test only.

decisions.title: Calendar Completion Passes Must Measurably Improve the Calendar
decisions.decision: Every implementation pass during CC-01–CC-12 must measurably improve calendar correctness, usability, interoperability, or operational reliability. Neutral refactors and unrelated campaign expansion are forbidden unless they directly enable and verify a calendar improvement.
decisions.boundary: Does not waive Architecture 1.0 ownership or Communications freeze.

decisions.title: CC-02 Integrity Console Detects Without Auto-Mutating Events
decisions.decision: The Calendar Integrity & Provenance Console (CC-02) surfaces duplicates, provenance gaps, import anomalies, drift, and lifecycle warnings through bounded scans and dispositions. Scans, views, and dispositions never automatically merge, delete, cancel, archive, restore, reschedule, or rewrite Events or Missions.
decisions.boundary: Time/recurrence corrections remain CC-03/CC-04. Explicit repairs require preview, auth, confirmation, and audit when later enabled.

decisions.title: Kelly CC-05-Only Waiver of Unfinished Usability Synthesis Gate
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly waives the unfinished Operator Usability Synthesis gate for Calendar Completion CC-05 only, authorizing the calendar-only Standing Availability Inputs slice (rules, exceptions, evaluation, overlays, create/reschedule warnings). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_05_WAIVER_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. Usability Synthesis remains EMPTY and is not marked complete by this waiver.
decisions.boundary: CC-05 must not automatically move, cancel, confirm, or resolve Events; must not mutate Missions or external calendars; must not create persisted Conflict Engine records. CC-06 remains separately gated and is not authorized by this waiver.

decisions.title: Post-CC-05 Next Pass Is Operator Usability Pass 1 and Synthesis 1
decisions.decision: With CC-05 shipped at main@46a72c3 / Netlify 6a60efa8f25804bc9b16f3f3, Operator Usability Pass 1 and Usability Synthesis 1 remain required and Synthesis remains EMPTY. Do not mark Synthesis complete because CC-05 shipped.
decisions.boundary: Usability observation remains OPEN. CC-06 engineering authorization is separately recorded in ADR-092 (2026-07-22); ADR-091 does not itself authorize CC-06 and does not complete Synthesis.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-06 Conflict Engine Calendar Slice
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-06 Conflict Engine — Calendar Slice (detect/persist explainable conflicts; consume CC-03/CC-04/CC-05; operator queue and dispositions). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_06_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. Separate from ADR-090. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete and must not be marked complete because of this authorization. CC-07 remains a separate deliverable.
decisions.boundary: CC-06 must never automatically move/cancel/confirm/archive/restore/delete Events; change Event status, recurrence, availability, Missions, Travel/Logistics/Field Ops/Staffing/Closeout/Launch; invent travel facts; mark ACKNOWLEDGED as resolved; resolve because an Event ended; or write to external providers. ACKNOWLEDGED does not clear a blocker.

decisions.title: Phase Two Intelligent Statewide Campaign Calendar Vision Locked After CC-12
decisions.decision: Kelly locks Phase Two (IC-01…IC-12) as the major post–Calendar Completion program: an intelligent statewide campaign operating calendar for Mission meaning, geographic/strategic gaps, volunteers/travel coordination, and mobile action. Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_PHASE_TWO_VISION_LOCK_KELLY_2026-07-22.md and develop_notes/KCCC_PHASE_TWO_INTELLIGENT_STATEWIDE_CAMPAIGN_CALENDAR.md. Protected sequence: CC-07→CC-08→CC-09→CC-10→CC-11→CC-12→usability/AI-quality gate→IC phase authorization. During CC-07…CC-12, clean extension points (query contracts, design tokens, mobile-safe layouts, provider-neutral interfaces, geographic identifiers) are allowed; hidden Phase Two features and widened data collection are forbidden. Deterministic services own facts/authorization/consent/coverage/conflicts/permissible actions; AI explains and recommends within confirmation boundaries.
decisions.boundary: Does not authorize IC implementation. Does not authorize RedDirt/Mobilize write-back, auto Event/Mission/assignment/contact mutation, or marking Usability Synthesis complete. AI must not invent travel or silently decide geographic priorities. CC-07 engineering is separately authorized by ADR-095 under standing execution ADR-094.

decisions.title: Standing Kelly Execution Authorization for Calendar Completion Ship Cycles
decisions.decision: Kelly authorizes Burt/Cursor to execute approved Calendar Completion build scripts (CC-07…CC-12, then usability/AI-quality gate) through the full ship cycle—inspect, plan, edit, forward-only migrations, validate, repair, commit, push main, Netlify deploy, monitor, correct, redeploy, verify live routes, record evidence—without routine confirmation. The build script itself is authorization. Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_STANDING_KELLY_EXECUTION_AUTHORIZATION_2026-07-22.md. One deliverable per script; next queued script may start without another auth exchange.
decisions.boundary: Does not authorize destructive production-data ops outside the script, credentials invention, purchases, material scope expansion, or Phase Two IC implementation before CC-12. Hard stops remain for privacy/legal/security decisions, unrecoverable conflicts, and production-data damage risk. Usability Synthesis remains EMPTY.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-07 Unified Search, Filters, and Saved Views
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-07 Unified Search, Filters, and Saved Views (permission-aware query contract, consistent filters, bookmarkable state, private/campaign-shared/role-restricted saved views; read-only CC-05/CC-06 consumption). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_07_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. Standing ship-cycle execution under ADR-094. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not implement CC-08 grid/drag/resize, bulk Event mutation, ICS/push/AI/RedDirt/Mobilize/volunteer/Phase Two features, or mutate Events/Missions/availability/conflicts/recurrence/imports/external systems from search/filter/saved-view reads.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-08 Advanced Day/Week Scheduling Workspace
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-08 Advanced Day/Week Scheduling Workspace (grid-first Day/Week; all-day lane; deterministic overlap layout; CC-05/CC-06 overlays; Event inspector; intentional slot creation; CC-07 query integration). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_08_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. CC-07 accepted complete at a630c8c / deploy 6a61167b80d9714ef4541631. Standing execution under ADR-094. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not implement drag-and-drop or resize mutation, bulk operations (CC-09), ICS/push/AI/RedDirt/volunteer/Phase Two features, or view-triggered Event/Mission/availability/conflict mutations. Intentional create/edit only via canonical Event services.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-09 Bulk Operations, Archive/Restore, and Recovery
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-09 Bulk Operations, Archive/Restore, and Recovery (multi-select, server-authoritative preview, archive/restore/cancel, limited calendar membership actions, idempotent execution, partial-failure reporting, safe recovery). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_09_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. CC-08 accepted complete at e1ddaa7 / tip deploy 6a612111e81d923c5e6c58ca (feature 7486aa9 / 6a611dc19547e64f0fa7874d). Standing execution under ADR-094. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not hard-delete Events, silently mutate Missions, bulk-reschedule, bulk-resolve conflicts, write external calendars/Mobilize, implement CC-10 ICS, or Phase Two IC features. Selection and preview must never mutate.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-10 ICS Export and Subscription Privacy
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-10 ICS Export & Subscription Privacy (one-time export, private signed subscription feeds, privacy profiles BUSY_ONLY/CITY_ONLY/OPERATIONAL_REDACTED, token hash-at-rest, rotate/revoke, ETag/304, rate limits, RFC 5545 subset). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_10_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-22.md. Migration 20260722180000_cc10_ics_export_subscription. Standing execution under ADR-094. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not offer public anonymous feeds (ADR-082), emit exact residential/street addresses in ICS (ADR-084), hard-delete Events/Missions, implement Google write-back/two-way sync, implement CC-11 Health Dashboard, or Phase Two IC features. CC-09 remains COMPLETE.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-11 Calendar Health Dashboard and Forensic Automation
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-11 Calendar Health Dashboard & Forensic Automation (bounded health runs, findings/alerts/checkpoints, operator dashboard, secret-authenticated scheduled ingress, redacted export). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_11_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-23.md. Migration 20260723100000_cc11_calendar_health. Standing execution under ADR-094. Observe/explain only. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not auto-repair/merge/delete Events or Missions, write automaticallyResolved on conflicts, rotate/revoke ICS feeds, implement Phase Two IC features. CC-10 remains COMPLETE. CC-02 integrity dispositions remain operator-owned. CC-12 authorized separately under ADR-100.

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of CC-12 Mobile Hardening, Print Day Sheets and Accessibility
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Calendar Completion CC-12 Mobile Hardening, Print Day Sheets & Accessibility (mobile-safe layouts, print projections with privacy profiles, accessibility hardening, operator print routes). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_CC_12_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-23.md. No CalendarPrint* migration. Standing execution under ADR-094. Presentation/usability only. After technical COMPLETE, human usability gate remains PENDING before Phase Two IC authorization. Usability Synthesis remains incomplete.
decisions.boundary: Must not migrate CalendarPrint* models, mutate Events/Missions from print/mobile chrome, reopen CC-11 auto-repair or ICS privacy defaults, add push/gcm_sender_id, expose print routes on public-paths, or implement Phase Two IC-01…IC-12. CC-11 remains COMPLETE.

decisions.title: Kelly Product-Owner Acceptance of CC-01–CC-12 Baseline and Post–CC-12 Gate Resolution
decisions.decision: Kelly accepts the completed Calendar Completion baseline (CC-01…CC-12) for operational use after using/reviewing the live calendar through CC-12, and directs engineering to fix whatever is needed to move forward. Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_POST_CC12_PRODUCT_OWNER_ACCEPTANCE_AND_PHASE_TWO_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-23.md. Human usability gate Result = ACCEPTED_BY_PRODUCT_OWNER_WITH_CONTINUING_OBSERVATION. Operator Usability Pass 1 remains blank and Synthesis 1 remains EMPTY as truthful facts — not fabricated multi-operator research. Structured pass continues as ongoing improvement evidence. Supersedes Synthesis as a blocking invent-to-pass gate for Phase Two foundation.
decisions.boundary: Must not invent usability session observations or mark Synthesis complete. Does not authorize IC-02…IC-12, OpenAI feature enablement, RedDirt/Mobilize integration, or Event/Mission auto-mutation. Per-IC authorization still required (ADR-102 for IC-01).

decisions.title: Kelly Authorization of IC-01 Arkansas Campaign Geography Foundation
decisions.decision: Kelly explicitly authorizes Phase Two IC-01 Arkansas Campaign Geography Foundation only (deterministic statewide geography graph, provenance-tagged priorities, validators, documentation, and ship cycle under ADR-094). Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_IC_01_AUTHORIZATION_KELLY_2026-07-23.md. Prerequisites: ADR-101 owner acceptance and ADR-103 AI quality foundation gate. IC-01 has zero OpenAI calls.
decisions.boundary: Must not call OpenAI, integrate RedDirt or Mobilize, build people/volunteer identity platform, silently auto-set geographic priority as campaign policy, create Events or Missions from geography features, or implement IC-02…IC-12. Those remain separately sequenced.

decisions.title: Phase Two AI Quality Gate Accepted for Foundation
decisions.decision: Phase Two AI quality gate is reviewed and accepted for Phase Two foundation (Status REVIEWED_AND_ACCEPTED_FOR_PHASE_TWO_FOUNDATION). Locks deterministic-facts / no-silent-mutation / server-only OpenAI / schema validation / confirmation boundaries / sixteen evaluation categories. Evidence: develop_notes/KCCC_PHASE_TWO_AI_QUALITY_GATE.md and develop_notes/KCCC_PHASE_TWO_AI_EVALUATION_STANDARD.md. Foundation IC work may begin; each AI-using feature requires its own eval before enablement. IC-01 has zero OpenAI calls.
decisions.boundary: Must not treat foundation acceptance as OpenAI production enablement. Must not skip per-feature eval, kill switches, or schema validation. Must not allow unrestricted DB access or client-side OpenAI keys.
